content: Spent the morning at Estrela reshooting Ana's spring menu. South light through the front windows, a single Profoto B10 for fill, everything else bounced off butcher paper. Twenty-five frames, no styling tricks — just the food, warm and close.
excerpt: A morning reshooting Estrela's spring menu in window light.
title: Behind the scenes: Estrela's spring menu reshoot

content: How I build a plate before the camera ever fires. Negative space first, then texture, then the one hero element. Earth tones, matte surfaces, nothing shiny that fights the light. Tweezers and a damp brush do most of the work.
excerpt: My approach to styling a plate before the shutter clicks.
title: How I style a plate for the camera

content: Three things restaurant work taught me. Light moves fast at service, so I shoot tethered into Capture One and chase it. The kitchen is the client — feed them frames as you go. And the half-day rate exists for a reason: rush the food and it shows.
excerpt: What years of restaurant shoots taught me about light and trust.
title: Three lessons from years of restaurant work

content: Big news: I'm shooting Chef Ana's cookbook, "Estrela: A Table in Brooklyn." Months of Portuguese-Brazilian dishes in the same warm Williamsburg light I've loved since the first menu shoot. More as the pages come together.
excerpt: Announcing the cookbook shoot with Chef Ana of Estrela.
title: Announcing: shooting the Estrela cookbook

content: Field notes from the Flushing night market for my "Night Markets of NYC" project. Steam, neon, dumplings under bare bulbs. Handheld, high ISO, leaning into the grain. Grew up eating here — this one's personal.
excerpt: Field notes from shooting the Flushing night market.
title: Field notes: Night Markets of NYC, Flushing

content: Why I rebuilt my window-light setup this spring. South-facing glass, sheer diffusion, and a single bounce — that's the whole kit most days. Draft for now; still testing how it holds up on overcast mornings before I publish.
excerpt: Rebuilding my window-light setup for soft, even days.
title: Rebuilding my window-light setup
